framework.name: Capability-Context Separation Principle
framework.description: Distinguishes capability envelope (model + tools + permissions, changes rarely, requires re-auth) from runtime context (conversation, user input, changes per-interaction, requires auditability). Argues existing frameworks conflate these — a tool addition and a user message are treated identically. This is the theoretical justification for why pure OAuth composition is insufficient.
framework.raw_quote: Existing frameworks authenticate who an agent is and authorize what it may access, but none detect when the agent's capabilities have changed. This gap enables a class of attacks we term silent capability escalation.

source.title: Cryptographic Binding and Reproducibility Verification for AI Agent ...
